assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_058",
  "is_optimal": false,
  "approve": true,
  "feedback": "The remediation successfully uninstalled vsftpd and the OpenSCAP scan no longer reports the vulnerability.",
  "concerns": [
    "The script assumes the package is installed; if it is not, rpm -q will fail and the removal step may be skipped silently.",
    "No explicit handling of package dependencies beyond --noautoremove, which may leave unused dependencies.",
    "No logging or output to confirm removal success beyond exit codes."
  ],
  "suggested_improvements": [
    "Add a check for package presence before removal and provide clearer status messages.",
    "Consider using 'dnf remove -y vsftpd' without --noautoremove if you want automatic cleanup of unused dependencies.",
    "Include a rollback plan or verify that critical services are not impacted before removal."
  ],
  "security_score": 9,
  "best_practices_followed": true
}

best_practices_followed boolean: maybe false because script uses --noautoremove but still may leave orphan dependencies; also caution about testing; but generally follows recommended approach.
